title: R2 Carry-Forward Registry (§4.2)
description: Schema for runs/_r2_carry_forward.json. Tracks every R2-non-actionable item that closes the round under reduced-lifecycle (a + e). Two top-level statuses per pre-r2 kickoff checklist v1.4 §1.0: 'blocked' = waiting on at least one closed-enum unblock token; 'scheduled' = no external trigger, just round-scheduled (carries empty blocked_on). The 10-token closed enum and the AND/OR semantics are pinned at v1.4 LOCK; adding a token requires schema bump. Per-record uniqueness on item_id is unenforceable in draft-07 (uniqueItems compares full records); writers/verifiers MUST call scripts/_r2_schema_utils.py::validate_and_enforce(records, schema_path, unique_keys=['item_id']) — see UNIQUENESS_KEYS in _r2_schema_utils for the canonical mapping.

item_id.description: Stable identifier for the carried-forward item, conventionally referencing the source plan section (e.g. 'kd_first_cell', 'tsm_phase_1a', 'sahi_inference_only', 'multi_cam_fusion'). Writer-enforced uniqueness across the array.

status.description: blocked = waiting on at least one external trigger from the closed enum. scheduled = round-internal scheduling only (no external trigger; blocked_on must be empty).

blocked_on.description: Closed-enum unblock tokens. Single-token = unblock_logic moot (omit). Multi-token = AND by default; explicit unblock_logic='any' switches to OR. status='scheduled' forces blocked_on=[] (enforced below). Closed enum locks the unblock vocabulary; new triggers require schema bump.

unblock_logic.description: Semantics for multi-token blocked_on. 'all' = every token must clear (default; redundant to write but allowed). 'any' = unlock when any single token clears (must be explicit per v1.4 LOCK iter-3 ruling: unencoded OR was undefined behaviour pre-iter-3). Forbidden when status='scheduled'.

unblock_evidence_path.description: Optional. Repo-relative POSIX path mirroring blocking_artifacts pattern in _r2_component_decision_schema.json. Forbids: absolute Unix (^/), Windows drive prefix (^[A-Za-z]:), leading current-dir (^./), backslashes, double slashes (//), '.' as any path segment, '..' segments, trailing slash, and whitespace (no prose smuggling). Hidden files allowed (leading dot in name is fine). Empty string rejected via [^\s]+. Used for documentation that proves the unblock condition has been (or will be) verified — e.g. a planning doc anchor, a sidecar manifest path, a transcript reference. When present, _r2_verify.py is expected to assert path-existence (filesystem state, not schema-checkable).

next_entrypoint.description: Free-text or path-like identifier of the resumption point once unblocked (e.g. 'additional_components_plan §七.6', 'temporal_optimization_plan §1.5.4 b-stage', 'r3_latency_budget_release'). Empty disallowed: a carry-forward record without a resumption pointer is a black hole.
